activities.title: Transfer to Airport

metro_pass.note: Valid for 72 hours on metro, buses, and trams

notes: Rome's historic center is very walkable
Most attractions are within walking distance
Metro has two main lines (A and B) that intersect at Termini
Validate tickets before boarding public transport

booking_essentials.booking_window: Book major attractions immediately, at least 3-4 weeks before travel

money.payment: Credit cards widely accepted, but carry cash for small cafes and markets

cash_recommended.uses: Street food, markets, tips, small purchases

money.tipping: Service charge often included; round up or leave 5-10% for good service

best_practices.tip: Lunch is typically 12:30-14:30, dinner starts at 19:30 or later
Visit popular sites early morning or late afternoon to avoid crowds

best_practices.rule: Shoulders and knees must be covered for churches (bring a scarf)

best_practices.tip: Drink from the nasoni (public fountains) - water is safe and free

best_practices.note: Cappuccino is a breakfast drink; after 11am, order espresso

best_practices.tip: Happy hour (18:00-20:00) often includes free snacks with drinks

safety: Watch for pickpockets on public transport and at tourist sites
Keep valuables in front pockets or inside bags
Be cautious of distraction scams near major attractions
Use ATMs inside banks during business hours when possible

food_tips.breakfast: Italian breakfast is light - coffee and cornetto (croissant)
food_tips.aperitivo: 6-8pm tradition of drinks with complimentary snacks
local_specialties: Cacio e pepe - pasta with pecorino and black pepper
Carbonara - pasta with egg, guanciale, and pecorino
Supplì - fried rice balls with mozzarella
